back_translation: Rehoboam went to Shechem, for all Israel had come there to make him king.
When Jeroboam son of Nebat heard this—he was in Egypt, where he had fled from King Solomon—Jeroboam returned from Egypt.
So they sent and called him, and Jeroboam and all Israel came to Rehoboam and said,
“Your father put a heavy yoke on us. Now lighten the hard service of your father and the heavy yoke he put on us, and we will serve you.”
Rehoboam said, “Come back to me again in three days.” So the people went away.
King Rehoboam consulted the elders who had served Solomon his father while he was alive, asking, “How do you advise me to answer these people?”
They answered, “If you are kind to these people and please them and speak gentle words to them, they will be your servants forever.”
But Rehoboam rejected the advice the elders gave him, and consulted the young men who had grown up with him and served him.
He asked them, “What do you advise that we answer these people who said to me, ‘Lighten the yoke your father put on us’?”

The young men who had grown up with him said, “Say to the people who said to you, ‘Your father made our yoke heavy, but you lighten it,’ tell them: ‘My little finger is thicker than my father's waist.
My father put a heavy yoke on you, but I will add to your yoke. My father scourged you with whips, but I will scourge you with scorpions.’”
After three days, Jeroboam and all the people came back to Rehoboam, as the king had said, “Come back to me on the third day.”
And the king answered them harshly. King Rehoboam rejected the advice of the elders
and spoke to them according to the advice of the young men, saying, “My father made your yoke heavy, but I will add to it. My father scourged you with whips, but I will scourge you with scorpions.”
So the king did not listen to the people, for this turn of events was from God, that the LORD might fulfill the word he spoke through Ahijah the Shilonite to Jeroboam son of Nebat.

When all Israel saw that the king would not listen to them, the people answered the king, “What portion do we have in David? And what inheritance in the son of Jesse? To your tents, O Israel! Now look to your own house, O David!” So all Israel went to their homes.
But Rehoboam still reigned over the Israelites who lived in the cities of Judah.
Then King Rehoboam sent out Hadoram, who was in charge of the forced labor, but the Israelites stoned him to death; and King Rehoboam hurried to mount his chariot and fled to Jerusalem.
So Israel has been in rebellion against the house of David to this day.
